title: Global Value Chain Decomposition with decompr

title: Global Value Chain Decomposition with decompr
text: leontief() pre-multiplies Leontief inverse value-added coefficient matrix post-multiplies exports. result gives value-added origin (rows) exporting country-industry (columns). row identifies source country-industry, using country-industry, amount value added source embodied using sector’s exports. Setting long = FALSE returns underlying matrix directly.

title: Global Value Chain Decomposition with decompr
text: kww() aggregates country level splits exports 9 components: domestic value added (DVA), foreign value added (FVA), various double-counting terms. Note: KWW decomposition contains known systematic bias — underestimates foreign value added conflating domestic double-counting. Use bm(perspective = "world", approach = "sink") Borin-Mancini correction (see ).

title: Global Value Chain Decomposition with decompr
text: wwz() operates bilateral country-industry level decomposes exports 16 value-added double-counting terms importing country. result long-format data frame one row per (exporting country-industry, importing country) pair one column per term, preceded three identifier columns (Exporting_Country, Exporting_Industry, Importing_Country). Columns beyond 16 decomposition terms accounting diagnostics (texp, texpint, texpfd, etc.). wwz2kww() maps 16-term result 9-term KWW format needed:

title: Global Value Chain Decomposition with decompr
text: bm() flexible decomposition. supports three aggregation levels ("country", "sector", "bilateral") two accounting perspectives ("exporter" 13 terms, "world" 9 terms).

title: Global Value Chain Decomposition with decompr
text: 13 terms break gross exports (GEXP) domestic content (DC) foreign content (FC), value-added double-counting components. GVC participation measured GVC = GEXP - DAVAX, split backward (GVCB) forward (GVCF) linkages.

title: Global Value Chain Decomposition with decompr
text: WWZ BM (exporter/source) decompose bilateral exports value-added double-counting components, disagree “double-counted” means — choice shapes questions method can answer. WWZ uses bilateral perimeter: item double-counted crosses s→r border . Compared BM, looser, items BM classifies double-counted appear value-added WWZ. makes WWZ well-suited questions GDP content specific trade flow — tariff incidence, bilateral trade balances — tradeoff WWZ results add : summing bilateral terms importers recover sensible country-level total. WWZ also conflates source-based sink-based approaches within decomposition, terms accounting footing GVC indicators DAVAX computed output. BM (exporter/source) uses exporting country perimeter: item double-counted second time crosses country s’s border, wherever goes. One approach throughout, 13 terms comparable. Bilateral results sum sector totals, sector totals country totals — DAVAX, GVC-related trade, backward/forward participation shares come calculation. country- sector-level work, BM (exporter/source) natural choice; reach WWZ question specifically crosses particular bilateral border.

GVC indicators based decompositions available companion gvc package.

what: Borin-Mancini Decomposition of Gross Exports — bm
title: Borin-Mancini Decomposition of Gross Exports — bm
text: Decomposes gross exports value-added Global Value Chain (GVC) components following Borin Mancini (2019) framework, implemented Stata icio command (Belotti, Borin Mancini 2021). R counterpart decompose() function Julia package ICIO.jl, operates decompr object created load_tables_vectors.

title: Borin-Mancini Decomposition of Gross Exports — bm
code: bm(   x,   aggregation = c("country", "sector", "bilateral"),   perspective = c("exporter", "world"),   approach = c("source", "sink") )

title: Borin-Mancini Decomposition of Gross Exports — bm
text: x object class decompr obtained load_tables_vectors. aggregation character. level decomposition: "country" (one row per exporting country), "sector" (one row per exporting country-industry), "bilateral" (one row per exporting country-industry importing country, excluding within-country flows). Default "country". perspective character. accounting perspective defining perimeter double counting: "exporter" (exporting-country perspective, additive across sectors destinations, 13 terms) "world" (world perspective, 9 terms, available aggregation = "country"). Default "exporter". approach character. double-counted items allocated across shipments: "source" (value added recorded first time leaves country origin) "sink" (last time). "exporter" requires "source"; "world" requires "sink". Default "source".

title: Borin-Mancini Decomposition of Gross Exports — bm
text: data.frame one row per unit one column per value-added term,  preceded factor identifier columns: Exporting_Country (country);  Exporting_Country, Exporting_Industry (sector); Exporting_Country,  Exporting_Industry, Importing_Country (bilateral). attribute "decomposition"  set "bm".

title: Borin-Mancini Decomposition of Gross Exports — bm
text: default exporter / source perspective decomposition yields 13 terms; world / sink perspective (country level , "corrected KWW" decomposition) yields 9. terms units input-output table (e.g. millions USD). following accounting identities hold: GEXP = DC + FC, DC = DVA + DDC, FC = FVA + FDC, DVA = VAX + REF, (exporter/source ) GVC = GVCB + GVCF = GEXP - DAVAX GVCB = FC + DDC. exporter / source decomposition additive: "sector" result sum "bilateral" result importers, "country" result sum "sector" result industries.

title: Global Value Chain Decomposition — decompr-package
text: Four global value chain (GVC) decompositions implemented. Leontief decomposition derives value added origin exports country industry Hummels, Ishii Yi (2001). Koopman, Wang Wei (2014) decomposition splits country-level exports 9 value added components, Wang, Wei Zhu (2013) decomposition splits bilateral exports 16 value added components. Borin Mancini (2019) decomposition splits country-, sector- bilateral-level exports 13 value added GVC components, also provides corrected version (biased) KWW decomposition. Various GVC indicators based decompositions computed complimentary 'gvc' package.

title: Global Value Chain Decomposition — decompr-package
text: Interface function quick analysis decomp() Function load ICIO table create 'decompr' object load_tables_vectors() Functions perform GVC decompositions 'decompr' object leontief()kww()wwz()bm() Function obtain KWW decomposition WWZ decomposition wwz2kww() Example ICIO data data("leather")

Tracing value-added double counting gross exports. American Economic Review, 104(2), 459-94. Wang, Zhi, Shang-Jin Wei, Kunfu Zhu (2013). Quantifying international production sharing bilateral sector levels (. w19677). National Bureau Economic Research. Borin, ., & Mancini, M. (2019). Measuring Matters Global Value Chains Value-Added Trade. World Bank Policy Research Working Paper 8804.

title: Koopman-Wang-Wei Decomposition of Gross Exports — kww
text: KWW decomposition known biased. shown Borin Mancini (2019), systematically underestimates foreign value added exports – correspondingly overstates foreign double counting – entire foreign content direct importer re-exports third countries classified 'foreign double counted', including part (value added generated importing country re-exported onwards) never recorded foreign value added flow. KWW also overlooks bilateral dimension trade, correctly split domestic value added absorption direct importer third markets (hence indicators DAVAX derived ). Borin Mancini (2019) correct issues using sink-based, world-level perspective foreign content exports; corrected KWW decomposition available bm(x, perspective = "world", approach = "sink").

TRUE sets inventory (last final demand category country) zero.

code: # Load example data data(leather)  # Create intermediate object (class 'decompr') decompr_object <- load_tables_vectors(leather)   # Perform the WWZ decomposition WWZ <- wwz(decompr_object)  # Obtain a disaggregated KWW decomposition KWW <- wwz2kww(WWZ)  # Aggregate KWW  wwz2kww(WWZ, aggregate = TRUE) #>     Country  DVA_FIN  DVA_INT DVA_INTrex   RDV_FIN   RDV_INT       DDC #> 1 Argentina 19.34940 18.97119   8.411491  5.259501 0.8259724 0.8723949 #> 2    Turkey 43.39461 26.20678   7.740053 10.475795 2.0075781 2.6369363 #> 3   Germany 78.73101 15.23967   2.748464  5.689669 4.4335916 4.4481800 #>     FVA_FIN  FVA_INT      FDC #> 1  3.450595 3.388617 3.770832 #> 2 10.205386 5.359698 5.573163 #> 3 26.668992 4.455024 5.185401  # Same as running KWW directly, but the former is more efficient  # if we already have the WWZ kww(decompr_object) #>     Country  DVA_FIN  DVA_INT DVA_INTrex   RDV_FIN   RDV_INT       DDC #> 1 Argentina 19.34940 18.97119   8.411491  5.259501 0.8259724 0.8723949 #> 2    Turkey 43.39461 26.20678   7.740053 10.475795 2.0075781 2.6369363 #> 3   Germany 78.73101 15.23967   2.748464  5.689669 4.4335916 4.4481800 #>     FVA_FIN  FVA_INT      FDC #> 1  3.450595 3.388617 3.770832 #> 2 10.205386 5.359698 5.573163 #> 3 26.668992 4.455024 5.185401

title: decompr 7.0.0
text: Added Borin-Mancini (2019) decomposition via new bm() function (also available decomp(method = "bm")). decomposes gross exports 13 value-added GVC terms country, sector, bilateral-sector level, exporter/source world/sink perspective. R counterpart decompose() Julia package ICIO.jl reproduces Stata icio command’s output. kww() documentation now notes KWW decomposition biased (systematically underestimates foreign value added) points bm(perspective = "world", approach = "sink") Borin-Mancini correction. Cross-references bm() added throughout.
